Association British Insurers' (ABI) most comprehensive analysis into insurance fraud published this week highlights that every minute insurance fraud detected
first time, ABI's annual detected fraud figures include data application fraud where details such age, address, claims history deliberately miss-stated.
total 562,000 insurance frauds were detected insurers 2017 these there were 113,000 fraudulent claims, 449,000 dishonest insurance applications.
number dishonest insurance claims, 113,000, were valued £1.3 billion.
number organised frauds, such staged motor accidents, fell 2016, with frauds worth £158 million detected.
This reflected work Insurance Fraud Bureau (IFB), currently investigating rising number suspected frauds, Insurance Fraud Enforcement Department (IFED).
IFED specialist police fraud unit investigating insurance fraud, such staged motor accidents illegal insurance advisers (so-called ghost brokers).
Since formation 2006, IFED secured over court convictions insurance fraud.
value fraudulent detected motor insurance claims, £775 million, rose 2016.
number these frauds, 67,000, showed small rise.
Fraudulent property insurance claims fell.
number detected dropped 2016 22,000, with value £100 million.
Insurers detected 449,000 cases confirmed suspected application fraud, where people lied withheld information cheaper cover.
Motor insurance made bulk dishonest applications, with typical lies including nature applicant's occupation, driving record, where previous claims motoring convictions were disclosed.
cyclist claimed £135,000 compensation from council injuries said sustained when fell cycle after hitting pothole.
However, evidence showed that accident happened when fell slippery road another location.
jailed three-and-a-half years.
another example, bodybuilder, claimed £150,000 back injury, exposed when filmed doing press-up challenge.
ordered £35,000 legal costs.
ring leader gang staged crash £500,000 insurance pay-outs fake injuries jailed banned from driving years.
Using rental car, staged crash, following which eight fellow fraudsters claimed fake injuries necks hips.
Finally student convicted after attempting claim £14,000 through invented claims following trip Venice, including alleged loss iPod, laptop designer watch.
only relatively recently, 2015, that Financial Ombudsman (FOS) shared their insight into banking complaints involving telephone fraud.
those days appeared that older people were more likely landlines meant they were particularly risk hang scams.
But, according their latest review today's connected world, it's often loopholes technologies, rather than ones, that fraudsters using their advantage.
highlight that first step toward being scammed individual putting their details into identical, fake banking website responding text message that, face looks like it's from their bank.
Unlike most other complaints see, complaints about fraud scams involve whether it's accepted suspected actions criminal third party.
it's understandable that, many cases, both bank their customer tell strong terms that they're responsible what's happened.
This makes harder reach answer that both sides happy with.
doesn't mean usual standards don't apply.
case studies from illustrate, they will expect clear evidence that banks have investigated thoroughly reflected hard what more might have been done protect their customers their money.
also often hear from banks that their customers have acted with "gross negligence" this means they're liable money their customer lost.
However, according FOS, gross negligence more than just being careless negligent.
their case studies show, evolution criminals' methods particular, their sophisticated technology manipulative "social engineering" means it's increasingly difficult case make.
there's anything salvaged wake fraud scams, it's what everyone learn about they happened what needs change.
Eating large meal could help detect early signs metabolic conditions such type diabetes, according research British Heart Foundation part-funded, published journal Cell Reports.
team researchers, Samuel Virtue Professor Toni Vidal-Puig Cambridge University Metabolic Research Laboratories Medical Research Council's (MRC) Metabolic Diseases Unit, have made observation whilst studying gene called PPARy2.
This gene controls formation function tissue, which stores energy form fat.
Researchers found that mice that lack PPARy2, lipids form free were sufficiently stored tissue were redirected other organs, which early sign metabolic disease diabetes.
Although young mice without PPARy2 looked 'healthy', they went develop insulin resistance (the process that underlies early diabetes) they older.
glucose tolerance test (GTT) diagnostic test used routinely detect diabetes.
glucose drink taken after period fasting test measures well body's cells able absorb glucose.
When team performed mice without PPARy2, results were similar normal mice.
when they replaced glucose with large, fatty meal equivalent eating Christmas dinner signs metabolic disease emerged, including times levels insulin found normal mice given same fatty meal, increased blood glucose increased blood fatty acids.
researchers believe this because PPARy2 especially important clearing free fats from blood quickly after high meal storing them inside tissue.
mice without PPARy2, tissue overwhelmed high meal lipids built blood were redirected other organs, eventually leading insulin resistance mice aged.
Together with Professor José Manuel Fernández-Real, University Girona, team also demonstrated that PPARγ2 levels humans lower obese individuals, demonstrating that their findings could also relevant humans.
